text: The discharge temperature should be 125°F and the suction pressure is 70 psig.
Normal operating range for suction pressure is 60-80 psig. Discharge pressure typically 200-250 psig.
The system superheat should reach 65 degrees, and subcooling around 10 degrees.
The ambient temperature was -10°C and pressure dropped to -5 psig.
Water temperature should be 40°C. System pressure is 150 kPa.
High discharge pressure of 300 psig indicates a problem. Temperature of 200°F at discharge.
Operating at approximately 135°F which is slightly above normal range of 120-130°F.
This document contains no numeric measurements relevant to the claim.
